Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health
Nurse-Family Partnership
About Nurse-Family Partnership
Better Worlds Start with Great Families!
Nurse-Family Partnership® is an evidence-based, community health program with 45 years of research showing Nurse-Family Partnership succeeds by having specially educated nurses regularly visit first-time moms, starting early in the pregnancy and continuing until the child’s second birthday. Research consistently proves that the partnership between a nurse and the mom is a winning combination that makes a measurable, long-term difference for the whole family.
Nurse-home visitor arriving to the home of a mother and baby
Program Goals:
Improve pregnancy outcomes by partnering with moms to engage in good preventive health practices, including thorough prenatal care from their healthcare providers, improving their diets and reducing any use of habit-forming substances.
Improve child health and development by assisting families to provide responsible and competent care; and
Improve the economic self-sufficiency of the family by supporting parents to develop a vision for their own future, plan additional pregnancies, continue their education and find work.
Who is Eligible for the Program?
Any woman who:
• Is less than 28 weeks pregnant with her first child
• Meets income requirements (eligible for WIC or Medi-Cal)
• Is a current Solano County resident
